_meta: Human-labeled calibration set for the SKILL-QUALITY judge. ONE JSON object per line; rows with a _meta key are skipped. Schema: {skillPath, dimension (D1-D6), humanScore (0.0-1.0), note}. Seeded 2026-07-21 (R. Rice via Claude) after the portability pass, spanning a spread: exemplary portability (session-review, todo) vs. tool-chain-heavy (skill-evolve, skill-scorecard); tight trigger/boundary (roast-me, session-review) vs. sibling-refs-not-present (technical-due-diligence). Both good and weak anchors present per dimension. Adjust any score you disagree with; grow as skills evolve.

note: Gold-standard portability anchor: explicitly a GLOBAL skill; every project-specific input (bug-patterns catalog, TODO.md, type-check, ledger) is framed 'consult whichever the project has (skip the absent ones)'; illustrative examples are labeled as such. Nothing hard-stops on a missing anchor.

Rich, concrete auto-activate triggers AND explicit skip conditions AND named boundaries vs siblings (/codeburn, /fix-loop); loses a touch only because it's long enough to risk trigger-list truncation.
Fixed five-question workflow with a hard report-first->approval-gate->fix->verify->record order; the gate it promises in the description is actually enforced in Phase 2; concrete output format block.
Fully self-contained: reads/writes one repo-root TODO.md, no MCP, no external scripts, no generated-data dependency.
Description states the job, the default behavior, and the exact lane ('THE answer to check the todo / add to the todo'); minimal shadow risk.
Many real trigger phrasings plus a scoped negative (repo-wide audit belongs elsewhere) and a live evals/trigger_evals.json backing D1.

Post-fix: grep-code.mjs now auto-detected with a raw-grep fallback, description/areas genericized. Still assumes a Playwright src/ layout, so it degrades-and-states rather than fully adapting - a deduction, not a failure.

Portability preamble added (manual grep fallback for skill:graph/impact), but every phase body still leads with npm run skill:* as the primary path - the reader must lean on the preamble to run it anywhere else. Sound but tool-chain-forward.
Depends on sibling /skill-creator (present in eval-judge) - good - but also /sync and several _shared/scripts refs; now framed optional, yet a couple read as required handoffs. Middle of the range.

Description names /bug-gap-analysis, /gap-analysis, /coverage-gap-scan, /spec-review as negative-routing siblings - none exist here; now labeled project-specific in the preamble, so not a hard break, but the refs are still dead as written.
Preamble makes the _shared output-kit and dispatch template optional with a from-scratch HTML fallback; a strong recovery, but the body's default instructions still assume the kit first.

KB cites old-project paths/emails/_shared files, but these are now explicitly labeled a personal historical ledger the skill never dereferences (exempt per rubric); the self-referential absolute path was fixed to relative. Sits mid because a reader must trust the provenance framing.
Clear phase map (0-7) with per-phase produces; non-negotiables stated up front; rubric-is-law contract. Coherent and well-ordered.

Portability preamble handles skill discovery across .claude/skills + plugins/*/skills and degrades to intrinsic-only without the graph; but signals/dispatch still center on _shared assets, and the empty seed was just introduced - solid, not exemplary.
Good boundary clause (do NOT use as a replacement for /three-amigos or /ready-for-testing) but fewer positive trigger phrasings than its peers; fires reliably for adversarial-review requests.
Core review logic is project-agnostic; input-doc locating now accepts an explicit path when the _shared/tickets layout is absent, with an ask-for-path fallback instead of a hard stop.
